message:
test: rewrite notify regression, add worker storage/docs/SDK contract assertions

Notify regression (notify_bug.rs):
- Rewrite test as a proper spurious-wakeup detector instead of a
  lost-wakeup test. The previous test verified that notify_one +
  notify_waiters didn't lose a token, but the actual bug surface is
  that dropping a broadcast-woken waiter could spuriously wake a
  sibling. The new test creates two futures, broadcasts to both,
  drops the first, and asserts the second does NOT become ready
  (spurious wakeup = regression). Also modernize NoopWaker (add
  wake_by_ref, use Arc::into() conversion).

WASM browser feasibility matrix:
- New test: worker_docs_distinguish_fail_closed_direct_runtime_from_
  bounded_package_helpers — asserts that the API surface census and
  TypeScript topology docs preserve the distinction between fail-closed
  direct runtime and bounded broker/coordinator helpers.
- Updated browser_sdk_runtime_contexts test: the browser SDK now
  preserves explicit "service_worker" and "shared_worker" runtime
  context values for truthful fail-closed diagnostics, instead of
  collapsing them into "unknown".

Contract pinning tests:
- wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract: pin new README WASM.md marker.
- wasm_next_adapter_lifecycle_contract: pin globalObject threading,
  nextClientBrowserRuntimeOptions helper, and all forwarded options.
- wasm_react_example_contract: pin globalObject threading through
  detectReactRuntimeSupport and ReactRuntimeProvider.
- wasm_rust_browser_example_contract: pin worker storage capability
  assertions (IndexedDB=true, localStorage=false).
- rust-browser-consumer/check-browser-run.mjs: assert storage
  capabilities in service-worker and shared-worker fail-closed ladders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
